title: GitHub Review by sourcery-ai[bot]
summary: In `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_database.go` (around line 501-510), the reviewer wants the `toDelete` and `toCheck` slices in `finalizeDatabaseResources` consolidated to share a single source of MariaDB resource definitions (e.g., a slice of constructors), preventing drift when resource types are added or removed.

code_comments.author: sourcery-ai[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Replaced the separate `toDelete` and `toCheck` literal slices in operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_database.go with a single `mariaDBResourceCtors []func() client.Object` defined once at the top of finalizeDatabaseResources. The Delete loop constructs a fresh object per ctor and sets name/namespace via SetName/SetNamespace; the Get loop constructs empty objects from the same ctors — so Database, User, and Grant are declared in exactly one place.

resolution_summary: Addressed the single review comment by centralizing the MariaDB resource kinds in finalizeDatabaseResources via a shared `mariaDBResourceCtors` slice of `func() client.Object` constructors. Both the Delete and Get phases now iterate over this shared slice instead of maintaining separate hard-coded `toDelete` and `toCheck` lists, eliminating drift risk when resource types are added or removed. No changes were made outside the file/location called out by the review.
